question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between enabled=false and the shouldDownload() bootstrap logic in AccountBalancesDownloader, where a fresh database causes shouldDownload() to return true regardless of the enabled flag, to force balance file downloads even in environments where the operator explicitly set enabled=false to disable balance ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.batchSize to Integer.MAX_VALUE via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getBatchSize() by the balance downloader, causing it to attempt to download an unbounded number of signature files per batch, exhausting memory and causing an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.consensusRatio=0 via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getConsensusRatio() by the balance downloader's signature verification, causing the downloader to accept balance files verified by zero nodes (no consensus required), allowing ingestion of unverified or forged balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.downloadRatio=0 via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getDownloadRatio(), causing the balance downloader to download from zero nodes, effectively halting balance file ingestion silently without any error being raised?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a malformed duration string (e.g., 'abc' or '30 seconds') as the value of hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency via an environment variable, causing Spring's Duration conversion to throw a ConversionFailedException during context startup, resulting in a denial-of-service by preventing the application from initializing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.timeout to PT0S (zero) via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getTimeout() by the balance downloader's HTTP client, causing all download requests to immediately time out, resulting in a denial-of-service where no balance files can ever be downloaded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathType=AUTO via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getPathType(), causing the balance downloader to perform runtime path detection per consensus node, and by poisoning the cloud storage bucket with files at both ACCOUNT_ID and NODE_ID paths, confuse the path detection logic and cause incorrect or duplicate file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set both writeFiles=true and writeSignatures=true via environment variables, causing the balance downloader to write both stream files and signature files to the filesystem on every download cycle, doubling disk I/O and storage consumption, potentially causing disk exhaustion in high-throughput environments?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].uri to an attacker-controlled SSRF target (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getSources() by the balance downloader's HTTP client, causing the importer to make requests to cloud metadata endpoints and potentially leak cloud credentials?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Data-generated setEnabled() setter being publicly accessible at runtime via Spring Boot Actuator's /actuator/beans or /actuator/configprops endpoints (if exposed), to discover that enabled=false is the current state and infer that balance data ingestion is disabled, leaking operational configuration details?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() returns StreamType.BALANCE which is used as a key in DateRangeCalculator's stream file repository map, and by causing the AccountBalanceFileRepository to return unexpected results (e.g., via a SQL injection in a connected component), manipulate the date range filter used for balance file downloads to re-download already-processed files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.maxSize=1 via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getMaxSize() by the balance downloader, causing all balance files larger than 1 byte to be rejected as oversized, effectively halting balance data ingestion and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to Long.MAX_VALUE milliseconds via a crafted ISO-8601 duration string, causing Duration.toMillis() to overflow to a negative value when passed to the @Scheduled fixedDelayString SpEL expression, resulting in a scheduler exception or unintended rapid polling?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathRefreshInterval to PT0S via environment variable, bypassing the @DurationMin(seconds=1) constraint if validation is not triggered on update, causing the consensus node path cache to refresh at zero-interval and flood the address book service with requests?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeFiles=true via environment variable and also set hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath to a network-mounted filesystem path, causing balance stream files to be written to a network share, potentially exposing sensitive balance data to other hosts on the network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].credentials.accessKey and secretKey to attacker-controlled values via environment variables, which are consumed through getCommon().getSources() by the balance downloader, causing the downloader to authenticate to cloud storage with attacker-provided credentials and potentially access an attacker-controlled bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring @ConfigurationProperties binding to set enabled=true via a YAML config file placed in the application's working directory (e.g., application.yml), which Spring Boot automatically loads, activating balance file downloads without any authentication or authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.cloudProvider=GCP and gcpProjectId to an attacker-controlled GCP project via environment variable, causing the balance downloader to bill download requests to the attacker's GCP project while reading from a potentially attacker-controlled GCS bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to a very small value (e.g., 1ms) via environment variable, causing the @Scheduled task in AccountBalancesDownloader to invoke downloadNextBatch() at near-zero intervals, creating a thundering herd of concurrent cloud storage requests that exhausts the connection pool defined by sources.maxConcurrency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].maxConcurrency to Integer.MAX_VALUE via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon().getSources() by the balance downloader's HTTP client, causing the client to open an unbounded number of concurrent connections to cloud storage, exhausting file descriptors and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteSignatures(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeSignatures=true via environment variable, causing signature files to be written to the filesystem, and if the importer process runs with elevated privileges, the written files inherit those privileges, allowing a local attacker to read or tamper with signature files that should be ephemeral?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.bucketName to an empty string via environment variable, causing CommonDownloaderProperties.getBucketName() to fall back to the network-derived bucket name, and if the network is set to 'other' with no hardcoded bucket, trigger an IllegalArgumentException at startup that prevents the application from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Component bean name is explicitly set to 'balanceDownloaderProperties', and if another bean with the same name is registered (e.g., via a test configuration or a classpath-scanned component), cause Spring to use the wrong bean, silently replacing the production BalanceDownloaderProperties with an attacker-controlled implementation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].backoff to PT0S via environment variable, causing the balance downloader to retry failed cloud storage requests with zero backoff, creating a tight retry loop that floods the cloud storage endpoint with requests and causes a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to a value that causes Duration.toMillis() to return exactly 0 (e.g., PT0.0009999S which rounds to 0ms), bypassing the @NotNull check since the value is non-null, causing the @Scheduled task to execute with a 0ms fixed delay and spin continuously, consuming all available CPU?
